questions.question: Explain the main difference between a one-dimensional array and a two-dimensional array.
questions.hint: A one-dimensional array is used to store elements in a single row or column, whereas a two-dimensional array is used to store elements in multiple rows and columns.

questions.question: How do you initialize a two-dimensional array in C++?
questions.hint: A two-dimensional array is initialized using the following syntax: int array[2][3];

questions.question: Write a C++ program to declare and initialize a two-dimensional array and print its elements.
questions.hint: Example: int array[2][3] = {{1, 2, 3}, {4, 5, 6}}; for (int i = 0; i < 2; i++) {for (int j = 0; j < 3; j++) {cout << array[i][j] << ' ';}}
